Petiton To 1) Invalidate The Irene M Lieberman Revocable Trust, Dated 6/16/2000 Amended And Restate
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Off Calendar

Cross-Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries And Instruct Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Oct-18-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: (Cases PES-15-298949 and PTR-15-299196 were heard jointly)
Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Richard Lieberman; Michael Loch (by CourtCall) for David and Paula Lieberman.
Mr. Zabronsky informed the Court that the parties had mediated their dispute at JAMS until late last night, and had reached a settlement of several but not all of their disputes. Under their agreement, the trust contest and will contest can be taken off calendar, but the petition for finding the will contests were without probable cause will remain on calendar in both the estate and trust proceedings. Court approval of the settlement will not be needed. The parties were jointly requesting the Court to continue the probable cause petitions in both matters only. Mr. Loch was in agreement. Upon agreement of counsel, the probable cause petition in each action was continued to October 18, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court directed that all submissions relating to those petitions be filed by no later than September 26, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Propounded On David Lieberman And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Propounded On Paula Lieberman And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Mar-23-2016 Text Ruling

Cross-Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries And Instruct Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Continued To Jun-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-15-298949 Estate of Irene Lieberman and PTR-15-299196 The Irene Lieberman Revocable Trust.
Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Michael Lieberman. Marissa Walter for Paula & David Lieberman who are present in court.

The Court asked counsel what was happening in the Civil Court. Ms. Walter stated that the civil court declined to combine the cases and that the elder abuse case will stay in civil. Mr. Zabronsky stated that they are proceeding on a parallel tract, that it may not remain that way, that the parties have talked, that they are not far apart as to the readiness for trial, that they can be ready in 12-18 months and that the decedent spoke of these matters to a large number of people. Ms. Walter stated that they could be ready for trial by December. The Court stated that it hoped it would not take a year, that this is not really a complex case, that there is a factual dispute regarding the circumstances in regards to signing papers, that it is inclined to set a date 6 months out, (9/6/16) with the expectation that the parties would be far along the way and have a firmer idea of what is left to do and asked how much time is needed. Mr. Zabronsky stated that they want a case management conference, that they believe the court should set for a short trial regarding the contest filed with probable cause and the main event to be determined based on all discovery. The Court stated that it will not set the date now and asked if depositions and discovery have taken place. Ms. Walter stated no. Mr. Zabronsky stated yes, largely and that there is a disagreement regarding facts known at the time of filing. The cross-petition for finding that petitioners, David and Paula Lieberman filed their will contest without probable cause and the request for order invalidating Irene M. Lieberman's will, etc., were continued to June 7, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Court Reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR #8052, 925-360-6065 (Reported)
